sessions.intent: Design and write test stubs for HTTP module (piece 4)

before.assumptions: Architect plan v1 approved. Existing test patterns from signing/crypto/identity modules provide templates.
before.risk: HTTP module has many interaction points (signing, providers, streaming, file I/O).

sessions.plan: 62 tests across 8 batches (52 C#, 10 PLang). Initial 51 from architect spec + 11 independent gap-coverage tests.

actions.context: 41 C# TUnit test stubs across 5 files
10 PLang test goals + 1 supporting goal
11 gap-coverage tests: timeout, OnProgress, null body, signed errors, application/plang+json, relative URL without BaseUrl, configure scope

after.status: Complete — 62 test stubs written (52 C# + 10 PLang)
after.notes: Initial 51 tests from architect spec, then 11 gap-coverage tests added independently (timeout, progress callbacks, signed errors, scope, edge cases). All stubs use Assert.Fail or throw placeholders.

sessions.intent: Implement HTTP module: 4 actions, provider, helper, tests

before.assumptions: Architect plan and test stubs are ready. Signing module exists and works via engine.RunAction.
before.risk: OBP compliance for signing integration, source generator enum defaults, application/plang response verification.

sessions.plan: Create 9 module files, modify 2 engine files, implement 54 C# tests, write 10 PLang goals. Fix source generator enum defaults.

actions.context: Full HTTP module: 4 actions, provider interface + default impl, shared helper
Register DefaultHttpProvider, add 'http' to ResolveType, add non-generic RunAction<TAction>()
Fix enum default values: emit (EnumType)intValue cast instead of raw int
Implemented all 54 test stubs with MockHttpProvider pattern
Wrote 10 PLang test goals + 1 supporting goal (can't build without LLM)

after.status: Complete — 9 module files, 2 engine mods, 1 generator fix, 54 C# tests pass, 10 PLang goals written
after.notes: OBP fix: signing result relayed as Data object (navigate .Signature), not decomposed. Added non-generic RunAction<TAction>() to Engine.

sessions.intent: OBP refactor: eliminate HttpHelper, provider owns all behavior

before.assumptions: HttpHelper static class violates OBP — behavior belongs to provider, not a static utility. Action records and IHttpProvider are clean.
before.risk: Large refactor of DefaultHttpProvider. Tests mock at IHttpProvider level so should be unaffected.

sessions.plan: Delete HttpHelper.cs. Move all methods into DefaultHttpProvider as private instance/static methods. Deduplicate error handling via ExecuteHttpAsync. Fix hardcoded progress var name. Eliminate duplicate redirect state. Add using on HttpResponseMessage. Finding #3 (signing record construction) deferred.

actions.context: Absorbed all HttpHelper methods. ExecuteHttpAsync deduplicates error handling. CreateClient reads from config scope chain (no duplicate state). Progress var name resolved from GoalCall params. Response disposed via using.
Static utility class eliminated — all behavior now owned by provider
Removed stale HttpHelper reference from doc comment
Replaced HttpHelper.ResolveUrl call in mock with direct Data.FromError

after.status: Complete — HttpHelper eliminated, all behavior in DefaultHttpProvider
after.health: 1875 pass, 8 skipped, 3 failures (pre-existing TestFixture DLL env issue)
after.notes: Finding #3 (signing record construction) deferred for discussion. All HTTP tests pass.

sessions.intent: Post-OBP polish: Transport [In], Settings.Apply, ISettings→IConfig rename

before.assumptions: OBP refactor done. Several improvements identified: application/plang parsing needed [In] for Signature, Configure had manual if-chain, ISettings naming inconsistent.
before.risk: ISettings→IConfig is a cross-cutting rename touching archive, signing, and http modules.

sessions.plan: 4 improvements: (1) Transport [In] attribute + TransportPropertyFilter for application/plang deserialization, (2) Clean up DefaultHttpProvider, (3) Settings.Apply reflection-based, (4) Rename ISettings→IConfig globally.

actions.context: TransportPropertyFilter handles [In] attribute for inbound deserialization. Data.Signature now populated from wire via [In]. application/plang response parsed as Data object directly.
Settings.Apply uses reflection to write action properties to scope chain — replaces manual if-chain in configure handlers.
Renamed ISettings→IConfig, module Settings→Config across archive, signing, http.
Updated tests for IConfig rename and Data.Signature changes

after.status: Complete — Transport [In], Settings.Apply, IConfig rename all applied
after.health: 1876 pass, 8 skipped, 3 failures (pre-existing TestFixture DLL env issue)
after.notes: Architect v2 plan (build-time defaults pinning) added but not implemented — scope for next piece.

sessions.intent: Design build-time defaults pinning for .pr files

before.assumptions: HTTP module complete. Defaults currently come from [Default] attributes which can change across runtime versions.

sessions.plan: Add Action.Defaults populated at build time. Source generator resolves: Parameters → Defaults → [Default] attribute. IConfigure<TConfig> for configure actions.

actions.context: Detailed design for build-time defaults: Action.Defaults, 3-tier resolution, IConfigure<T>, builder FillDefaults
Proposal documenting configure defaults split rationale and deferred decisions

after.status: Complete — design approved, ready for implementation
after.notes: Implementation deferred. Not blocking HTTP module completion.

sessions.intent: Add tests for TransportPropertyFilter and Settings.Apply — caught bug in filter

before.assumptions: TransportPropertyFilter and Settings.Apply had zero test coverage. Both are new components from coder-003.
before.risk: TransportPropertyFilter is security-critical — Data.Signature must round-trip for application/plang. Settings.Apply uses reflection to write scope chain.

actions.context: 7 tests: inbound deserialization, outbound serialization, roundtrip, null signature, default JSON exclusion, no-op on plain types
8 tests: non-null writes, null skipping, config-only properties, isDefault scope, nullable value types, dictionaries, multi-call merge

BUG FIX: CreateJsonPropertyInfo + Properties.Add does NOT override [JsonIgnore] in System.Text.Json. Fixed by removing existing hidden entries before re-adding with fresh Get/Set delegates. Data.Signature was silently never deserializing from application/plang responses.

after.health: 1891 pass, 8 skipped, 3 failures (pre-existing TestFixture DLL env issue)
after.notes: TransportPropertyFilter was broken since creation — Data.Signature never deserialized from wire. All application/plang response handling (ParsePlangResponseAsync, StreamPlangAsync) was silently getting null Signature. Fixed and verified via roundtrip test.

sessions.intent: Full 5-pass code analysis of HTTP, identity, signing, crypto, provider, config modules

before.assumptions: Coder completed HTTP module implementation across 3 sessions. Identity, signing, crypto, provider modules are new. Config was renamed from Settings.
before.risk: Large surface area — 30+ new files. HTTP provider is ~984 lines. Cross-module interactions (HTTP→signing→identity→crypto) create behavioral risks.

sessions.plan: Full 5-pass analysis: OBP, Simplification, Readability, Behavioral Reasoning, Deletion Test across ~35 new/modified source files.

actions.context: 5-pass analysis of all new/modified source files. Found 3 must-fix (provider disposal, catch-all, error swallowing), 2 should-fix, 2 minor, and untested streaming code.

after.health: 3 must-fix issues for coder, streaming coverage for tester
after.notes: Architecture is sound (OBP clean). Issues are in error handling and lifecycle management.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder fixes for v1 findings + analyze new disposal lifecycle and path resolution code

before.assumptions: Coder addressed all 7 v1 findings. Also added disposal lifecycle and path refactor.
before.risk: Fix-introduced code is the riskiest. New disposal lifecycle adds complexity to CallFrame and Engine.

sessions.plan: Verify all 7 v1 findings resolved. Full 5-pass on fix-introduced code: CallFrame disposal, Engine KeepAlive, Providers.All(), Path resolution, narrowed catches.

actions.context: All 7 v1 findings verified resolved. New code (disposal, path) is clean. One cosmetic issue (duplicate XML doc). No blocking issues.

after.health: Ready for tester. Carry-forward: streaming methods need C# test coverage.
after.notes: All findings resolved. New disposal lifecycle and path resolution are well-designed.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis for HTTP module (coder v2)

before.assumptions: 54 C# tests pass, 10 PLang goals exist. Coder reports all tests green.
before.risk: Mock-heavy test suites often hide real behavior gaps. 984-line provider is the primary risk.

sessions.plan: Run tests, collect coverage, analyze test quality with false-green hunting, write test-report.json

actions.context: All 54 HTTP tests use MockHttpProvider replacing real provider. Tests verify action delegation, not provider logic. 5 specific false greens identified.
13 findings: 1 critical, 7 major, 5 minor. Verdict: needs-fixes.

after.health: DefaultHttpProvider at 5.7% coverage. Send back to coder.
after.notes: Root cause: tests mock at IHttpProvider level instead of HttpMessageHandler level. Fix: TestableHttpProvider subclass with injected mock handler.

sessions.intent: Re-check coder fix + fresh-eye review of HTTP tests

before.assumptions: Coder rewrote tests with MockHttpMessageHandler per v2 feedback.
before.risk: Rewrite may have introduced new gaps or maintained weak assertions.

sessions.plan: Pull coder fix, run tests + coverage, verify v2 findings resolved, fresh-eye analysis of all test files

actions.context: All tests rewritten correctly with MockHttpMessageHandler. Coverage 5.7% → 88.3%. V2 critical/major findings resolved. 7 minor findings remain.
Updated report: verdict approved with 7 minor findings.

after.status: Complete — verdict FAIL (revised from initial PASS)
after.health: 3 false greens remain + form upload at 0%. Send back to coder.
after.notes: Initially approved but revised after self-challenge. 3 tests pass even if code is broken (auto-detect, JSON parsing, upload response). CreateFormContentAsync at 0% is a real user feature. Coverage improvement is real but assertions need strengthening.

sessions.intent: Fill HTTP provider test coverage gaps identified by tester v2

before.assumptions: Tester v2 rewrote tests to mock at transport level (MockHttpMessageHandler). 34 tests pass. Provider methods like ExecuteHttpAsync exception paths, streaming, header merging, signing, and form upload still at 0% coverage.
before.risk: Streaming tests rely on RunGoalAsync failing gracefully (no goal files in test). Signing tests require Ed25519Provider auto-registration to work.

sessions.plan: Add 25 tests: exception mapping (3), streaming (7), header merging (2), signed requests (4), form upload (4), config override (1). All use existing MockHttpMessageHandler pattern.

actions.context: Added 19 tests: exception mapping (HttpRequestException/IOException/FormatException), streaming (lines/SSE/bytes/error/custom-var/unsigned-plang), header merging (default+step/content-routing), signing (X-Signature header/valid JSON/plang response/invalid signature)
Added 4 tests: dict auto-detect form, explicit form, @file reference with filename, auto-detect object→JSON
Added 1 test: per-step TimeoutInSec=1 overrides configured TimeoutInSec=60

after.status: Complete — 25 new tests, 59 HTTP total, all pass
after.notes: Signing tests confirmed Ed25519Provider auto-registers and X-Signature roundtrip works. Streaming tests verified MemoryStack receives streamed data despite callback goal not found.

sessions.intent: Final re-check of HTTP tests after coder v3 fix + fresh-eye review

before.assumptions: Coder addressed 4 major findings: 3 false greens + form upload gap. Added 25 tests total.
before.risk: New tests may have their own false greens or weak assertions.

sessions.plan: Run tests + coverage, verify all v3-revised findings resolved, fresh-eye analysis of 25 new tests

actions.context: All 7 v3-revised findings resolved. 25 new tests are well-designed: strong assertions, behavioral verification, real signing integration. Coverage 95.9% line, 78.7% branch.
Final report: verdict approved, 3 non-blocking minors.

after.health: 95.9% provider coverage, 59 HTTP tests, all honest. Ready for security.
after.notes: 3 minor non-blocking: StreamWithProgressAsync 44.4%, TryExtractSignedErrorIdentity 0%, one weak Error.Key assertion.

sessions.intent: Security audit of HTTP module, provider system, crypto/signing, transport serialization

before.assumptions: HTTP module is new code on this branch. Provider registry, signing, crypto, identity modules exist from prior branches. Test coverage is at 95.9% per tester report.
before.risk: HTTP module handles external untrusted data (responses, headers, streaming). Signing integration is the trust boundary. Provider loading via Assembly.LoadFrom is by-design but worth documenting.

sessions.plan: Blue team + red team audit of HTTP module, provider registry, crypto/signing, transport serialization. 6 attack surface areas, 9 findings.

actions.context: Full HTTP module security review — response parsing, streaming, signing integration, upload content resolution
Signing/verification pipeline audit — 9-step VerifyAsync, ToSigningBytes, nonce replay, crypto
Provider registry, config scope chain, engine provider registration
Transport serialization — [In]/[Out]/[Sensitive] attribute system
Security audit deliverables — report, verdict, summaries

after.health: 1 high, 3 medium, 4 low. Systematic gap: no size limits on untrusted external data.
after.notes: Initially rated PASS with medium severities. Corrected after recognizing the response size issues are the HTTP module core responsibility, not hardening opportunities. Slow-drip attacks bypass timeouts.

sessions.intent: Re-audit after coder fixed all 4 blocking security findings

before.assumptions: Coder committed fixes for response size limits, SSE buffer cap, error body truncation, ToSigningBytes thread safety.
before.risk: Fixes may be incomplete or introduce new issues.

sessions.plan: Verify each of the 4 fixes: read the changed code, trace all call sites, check tests.

actions.context: Verified all 4 security fixes: size-limited reads, SSE buffer, error truncation, thread-safe signing
Verified tests cover oversized response, oversized binary, within-limit, concurrent ToSigningBytes
Updated security report to PASS, wrote v2 deliverables

after.health: 0 critical, 0 high, 0 medium open. All blocking findings fixed.
after.notes: Clean fixes. Minor observation: InvalidOperationException catch is slightly broader now but not blocking.
